publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury (with the Federal Reserve Board) — Treasury International Capital (TIC) reporting system
dataset_title: Treasury International Capital (TIC) System — U.S. International Portfolio Investment Statistics (monthly securities & banking data, plus annual/benchmark SHL and SHC surveys)
role_in_matrix: Supplies the bilateral dollar-asset layer of the FWTW matrix: foreign holdings of U.S. securities (U.S. liabilities) and U.S. holdings of foreign securities (U.S. claims), broken down by counterparty country, plus monthly Major Foreign Holders (MFH) of U.S. Treasuries by country. These provide the from-whom-to-whom cells for cross-border holdings of dollar (and U.S.-resident) securities.

structure.description: TIC publishes (a) MONTHLY securities data — monthly transactions in long-term securities between U.S. and foreign residents (covers both U.S. and foreign securities), monthly holdings of securities (foreign holdings of U.S. securities and U.S. holdings of foreign securities), and Section A 'Major Foreign Holders of Treasuries' (MFH table); (b) monthly U.S. banking data (liabilities to and claims on foreign residents), quarterly derivatives, quarterly nonfinancial data, and quarterly U.S. gross external debt; and (c) ANNUAL/BENCHMARK surveys: the SHL/SHLA survey of foreign holdings of U.S. securities (U.S. liabilities) and the SHC/SHCA survey of U.S. holdings of foreign securities (U.S. claims). Confirmed verbatim from the TIC home page text.
structure.monthly_vs_benchmark: CONFIRMED distinct. Monthly TIC securities data are higher-frequency, partly estimated, and custodial-based; the annual/benchmark surveys (SHL = annual foreign holdings of U.S. securities, as-of end-June; SHC = annual U.S. holdings of foreign securities, as-of year-end) are mandatory comprehensive surveys (TIC Forms SHL/SHLA and SHC/SHCA). Home page states annual surveys provide: line 1 U.S. holdings of foreign securities on a residence basis; line 1.a the same on a NATIONALITY basis; line 2 foreign holdings of U.S. securities; line 3 semiannual U.S. private holdings of foreign securities; line 4 end-year CLO and special tables. A new mandatory SHL survey of foreign holdings of U.S. securities as-of June 30, 2026 is announced (notice dated 06-10-2026). Latest final SHL: Foreign Portfolio Holdings of U.S. Securities at End-June 2025 (final report, press release 04-30-2026). Latest final SHC: U.S. Portfolio Holdings of Foreign Securities at Year-end 2024 (final report, press release 11-26-2025).
structure.counterparty_country_breakdown: CONFIRMED. MFH table (ticdata mfh.txt) lists holdings of U.S. Treasury securities by individual counterparty country (e.g. Japan, China Mainland, United Kingdom, Belgium, Luxembourg, Switzerland, Cayman Islands, Ireland, ...), an 'All Other' residual, a Grand Total, and a 'For. Official' split (Treasury Bills vs T-Bonds & Notes). Annual SHL/SHC surveys provide more comprehensive country-by-instrument detail. Residence basis for monthly and line-1/line-2 survey data; nationality basis additionally available for line 1.a of the annual survey.
structure.instruments: CONFIRMED. Covers long-term and short-term securities: U.S. Treasury bills, U.S. Treasury bonds & notes (marketable and non-marketable), and (via the broader securities reports/surveys) U.S. agency, corporate, and equity securities, and foreign securities held by U.S. residents. MFH footnote ties estimates to TIC Form SLT (Aggregate Holdings of Long-Term Securities) and Form BL2 (Customers' U.S. Dollar Liabilities to Foreign Residents).

access.method: Public, free, no authentication. Data files served from ticdata.treasury.gov/Publish/ as flat text/HTML files; descriptive/structural pages and press releases on home.treasury.gov. Data files retrieved successfully via direct HTTP (curl) returning HTTP 200; note WebFetch was blocked with HTTP 503 by Treasury's edge for home.treasury.gov pages, but the underlying content is publicly reachable.
access.api_or_bulk: Bulk flat-file download confirmed (ticdata.treasury.gov/Publish/*.txt returned 200, e.g. mfh.txt, slt1d.txt, mfhhis01.txt). No machine REST/JSON API was confirmed from the fetched pages — UNVERIFIED whether a formal API exists.

frequency: Mixed, CONFIRMED: monthly (securities transactions, securities holdings, MFH, banking liabilities/claims); quarterly (derivatives, nonfinancial, gross external debt); annual and semiannual benchmark surveys (SHL annual as-of end-June; SHC annual as-of year-end; line-3 semiannual).
reporting_lag: Monthly securities data: CONFIRMED empirically ~6 weeks (roughly 45 days after the reference month-end). Press release sb0536 reports data for April 2026 and states the next release, reporting May 2026 data, is scheduled for July 14, 2026 (i.e. May 2026 reference month published ~July 14, 2026). Annual surveys: long lag — final SHL report for the End-June 2025 reference date was published 04-30-2026 (~10 months); final SHC report for Year-end 2024 was published 11-26-2025 (~11 months).
coverage: Cross-border (residency-basis) holdings and transactions of securities between U.S. residents and foreign residents, by counterparty country; foreign official vs private split; U.S. Treasury, agency, corporate, equity, and foreign securities. Nationality-basis additionally available for U.S. holdings of foreign securities (annual survey line 1.a). Historical monthly archives and prior-survey reports retained.
known_frictions: CUSTODIAL / FINANCIAL-CENTER BIAS — CONFIRMED from MFH data file footnote 1/ (ticdata.treasury.gov/Publish/mfh.txt): 'The data in this table are collected primarily from U.S.-based custodians and broker-dealers. Since U.S. securities held in overseas custody accounts may not be attributed to the actual owners, the data may not provide a precise accounting of individual country ownership of Treasury securities.' This inflates holdings attributed to custodial hubs (Belgium/Euroclear, Luxembourg, Cayman Islands, Ireland, UK, Switzerland) relative to true beneficial-owner countries — a material distortion for FWTW bilateral attribution.

MONTHLY DATA ARE PARTLY ESTIMATED — MFH footnote states foreign holdings of Treasuries are 'based on' monthly Form SLT and Form BL2 data; monthly figures are benchmarked/revised against the annual SHL/SHC surveys, so monthly country cells are estimates pending survey reconciliation.

RESIDENCE vs NATIONALITY MISMATCH — most TIC series are residence-basis; only annual-survey line 1.a (U.S. holdings of foreign securities) provides a nationality basis. FWTW work mixing the two must reconcile bases.

ANNUAL-ONLY BENCHMARK FOR MOST GRANULAR COUNTRY-BY-INSTRUMENT DETAIL — comprehensive bilateral holdings come from annual SHL (end-June) / SHC (year-end) surveys with ~10-11 month publication lag, requiring temporal disaggregation/nowcasting to fit a higher-frequency matrix.

METHODOLOGY / FORM CHANGES — home page flags a TIC 'Article' dated 04-18-2024 and a 'Notice' dated 03-15-2023 affecting securities transactions and MFH series; TIC Form S ended in 2023; a new Continuous Securities Long-Term (CSLT) dataset was released 05-21-2026. These indicate ongoing form/series changes that affect series continuity (details of each change UNVERIFIED).

PUBLISHER EDGE BLOCKING — home.treasury.gov returns HTTP 503 to the WebFetch tool (automated fetch); content is reachable via direct HTTP with a browser user-agent. An automated ingestion pipeline must account for this.
unverified: Existence of a formal machine REST/JSON API (only bulk flat-file download confirmed).

The exact precise lag rule as stated by Treasury (lag of ~45 days inferred empirically from press release sb0536 schedule, not read from a stated rule on the JS-rendered Release Dates page, which did not render extractable schedule text).

The specific exact latest available MONTHLY vintage as of 2026-06-28 (the static ticdata cached files mfh.txt/slt1d.txt returned an older Jan-2023 vintage and appear stale/archived; press release sb0536 confirms April 2026 data is published and May 2026 data is due July 14, 2026, so the current monthly vintage is April 2026, but this was inferred from the press-release schedule rather than read off a live current data file).

The full substantive content of the 04-18-2024 TIC Article, the 03-15-2023 Notice, and the CSLT dataset methodology — their existence is confirmed but their detailed effect on series continuity was not read.

Detailed instrument taxonomy and country list of the annual SHL/SHC survey final reports (survey report pages not individually fetched; SHL/SHC structure summarized from the TIC home-page descriptive text only).
notes: Grounded against the live TIC home page and ticdata.treasury.gov data files on 2026-06-28. The custodial/financial-center bias and the monthly-vs-annual-benchmark distinction are CONFIRMED verbatim from the publisher (MFH footnote and home-page text). WebFetch was blocked (HTTP 503) on home.treasury.gov press-release and release-date pages; facts were instead obtained via direct HTTP fetch (curl) of the same publisher URLs plus the official press release sb0536. For FWTW assembly, the operative caveat is that monthly country-level Treasury holdings are custodial-attributed estimates that should be benchmarked to the annual SHL survey, and that financial-hub countries are over-weighted relative to beneficial owners.
